name: When I Work Scheduling API
what_it_does: Employee scheduling and time tracking platform with REST API for hourly and shift-based workforces — managing shift creation, employee availability, time clock punches, and schedule publication with mobile-first approach for retail, hospitality, and healthcare.
use_cases: Creating and publishing weekly shift schedules programmatically via API
Syncing time clock punch data to payroll systems via webhook or polling
Querying employee availability and time-off requests for automated scheduling
Building custom scheduling dashboards on When I Work attendance data
Automating shift swap approvals and notifications via API triggers
not_for: Salaried knowledge worker scheduling (use resource planning tools like Float)
Complex enterprise workforce management with multi-location analytics (use Kronos)
Project-based time tracking for billing (use Harvest or Toggl)
best_when: A small-to-mid-size business with hourly workers (retail, food service, healthcare) needs affordable shift scheduling with basic API integration for payroll sync.
avoid_when: You need complex scheduling rules, labor forecasting, or enterprise compliance tracking beyond basic shift management.

auth.notes: API token from account settings. Login endpoint returns token (not OAuth2). Token passed as Bearer in Authorization header. Account-level access — no fine-grained scopes. Token does not expire but can be revoked.

pricing.paid_tiers: Essentials from $2.50/user/month; Pro from $5/user/month; Enterprise custom
pricing.notes: Per-user monthly pricing for active employees. No free tier. US-based. API available on all paid plans. Strong SMB focus — retail, restaurants, healthcare. Mobile-first design. Integrates with Square, QuickBooks, ADP for payroll.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits apply; not publicly documented

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST API covering users, shifts, positions, locations, time clock, and attendance. Login-based token auth (not OAuth2). Webhook for shift and attendance events. Moderate documentation — covers core entities. Time zone handling required for shift start/end times.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Shift creation creates new shifts — not idempotent. User management idempotent by user ID. Read operations always idempotent. Time clock punches create new records.
known_agent_gotchas: Login endpoint returns token — not standard OAuth2 flow; must implement custom login
Time zones critical — shifts stored in account timezone, not UTC
Account ID required in many API URL paths
Webhooks require HTTPS endpoint and manual registration via UI, not API

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced. GDPR compliant. US data residency. Login-based token auth (weaker than OAuth2). No scope granularity — full account access per token. Standard SaaS security for SMB workforce tool.
